{"query":"FTX Chapter 11 plan confirmation date effective date plan of reorganization court filing January 2025 October 2024","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1753,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://restructuring.ra.kroll.com/ftx/","title":"Kroll Restructuring Administration","content":"You may unsubscribe by clicking the “Unsubscribe” link at the bottom of the email updates you receive.
On November 11, 2022 and November 14, 2022, FTX Trading Ltd. and 101 affiliated debtors (collectively, the "Debtors") each filed a voluntary petition for relief under Chapter 11 of the United States Bankruptcy Code in the United States Bankruptcy Court for the District of Delaware.
The Bankruptcy Court held a hearing to consider confirmation of the Plan on October 7, 2024, at 10:00 a.m.
On October 8, 2024, the Bankruptcy Court entered an order confirming the Plan.
On January 3, 2025, the Effective Date of the Plan occurred, and the Plan was consummated.
A hearing on the First Day Motions (the “First Day Hearing”) was held on November 22, 2022 at 11:00 a.m.

For transferred claims, distributions will only be made to the transferee holder of an allowed claim that is processed and reflected on the official register of claims maintained by the Notice and Claims Agent as of the January 3, 2025 record date, where the 21-day notice period has lapsed without objection."},{"url":"https://www.sullcrom.com/About/Rankings/2024/October/FTXs-14-Billion-Bankruptcy-Plan-Approved-Model-Case","title":"FTX’s $14+ Billion Bankruptcy Plan Approved in ‘Model Case’ | Sullivan & Cromwell LLP","content":"Less than two years after its international headline-grabbing collapse and historic bankruptcy filing, cryptocurrency platform FTX Trading’s $14+ billion Chapter 11 reorganization plan was approved.
It also required multiple complex settlements with competing creditor and customer groups, the IRS, the CFTC, the Department of Justice, numerous states and foreign governments, and seven other insolvency proceedings around world.
Confirmation was the capstone of this incredible effort.
a resolution of the $24 billion in claims filed by the IRS for periods prior to the Chapter 11 cases in return for a $200 million cash payment and a $685 million subordinated claim;

FTX.com) (\"FTX\") and its affiliated debtors (the \"FTX Debtors\") today announced its Court-approved Chapter 11 Plan of Reorganization (the \"Plan\") will become effective on January 3, 2025, which has also been set as the initial distribution record date for holders of allowed claims in the Plan's Convenience Classes (the \"Initial Distribution\").
